what: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R
title: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R
text: easyRasch2 CRAN-targeted R package provides functions Rasch measurement theory analysis workflows. successor easyRasch, offering lightweight, CRAN-ready structure proper namespacing minimal dependencies. Key functions implemented produce simulation-based critical values (cutoffs) conditional item infit MSQ Yen’s Q3 residuals evaluate local dependencies. materials Rasch analysis, see vignette package easyRasch.

title: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R
text: Estimation: Conditional Maximum Likelihood (CML) via eRm, psychotools, iarm item parameters; Weighted Likelihood Estimation (WLE) person parameters; mirt (MML) CML alternatives exist (e.g., Q3 residual correlations). Output: knitr::kable() tables (Quarto-friendly) ggplot2 figures, "dataframe" output options processing. Naming: Functions use RM prefix (e.g., RMlocdepQ3()).

title: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("pgmj/easyRasch2")

title: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R
code: library(easyRasch2)  # Simulate binary item response data set.seed(42) d <- as.data.frame(   matrix(sample(0:1, 200 * 10, replace = TRUE), nrow = 200, ncol = 10) ) colnames(d) <- paste0("Item", 1:10)  # Derive a simulation-based Q3 cutoff (500 iterations by default) cutoff_res <- RMlocdepQ3cutoff(d, iterations = 100, parallel = FALSE, seed = 42)  # Q3 table with simulation-based dynamic cut-off RMlocdepQ3(d, cutoff = cutoff_res$suggested_cutoff)  # Derive a simulation-based conditional item MSQ infit cutoff simfit <- RMinfitcutoff(d, n_cores = 4)  # Infit table with dynamic cut-off RMiteminfit(d, simfit)  # Infit plot with distribution of simulated MSQ values together with observed values RMinfitcutoffPlot(simfit, d)  # Item-restscore using Goodman-Kruskal's gamma RMitemrestscore(d)

what: Simulation-Based Infit MSQ Cutoff Determination — RMinfitcutoff
title: Simulation-Based Infit MSQ Cutoff Determination — RMinfitcutoff
text: Uses parametric bootstrap simulation determine appropriate cutoff values RMiteminfit. function simulates data correctly fitting Rasch model mimics data returns per-item empirical cutoffs.

title: Simulation-Based Infit MSQ Cutoff Determination — RMinfitcutoff
code: RMinfitcutoff(   data,   iterations = 250,   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = NULL,   cutoff_method = "hdci",   hdci_width = 0.999 )

title: Simulation-Based Infit MSQ Cutoff Determination — RMinfitcutoff
text: data data.frame matrix item responses. Items must scored starting 0 (non-negative integers). complete cases (rows without NA) used. iterations Integer. Number simulation iterations (default 250). parallel Logical. Use parallel processing via mirai available (default TRUE). n_cores Integer NULL. Number parallel workers. NULL, getOption("mc.cores") checked first. neither set parallel = TRUE, warning issued execution falls back sequential (single core) processing. verbose Logical. Show progress bar (default FALSE). seed Integer NULL. Random seed reproducibility. cutoff_method Character string specifying cutoff intervals computed. Either "hdci" (default) Highest Density Interval via ggdist::hdci(), "quantile" 2.5th/97.5th percentiles via stats::quantile(). hdci_width Numeric. Width HDCI cutoff_method = "hdci". Default 0.999 (99.9% HDCI). Ignored cutoff_method = "quantile".

title: Simulation-Based Infit MSQ Cutoff Determination — RMinfitcutoff
text: list components: results data.frame columns iteration, Item, InfitMSQ, OutfitMSQ (one row per item per successful iteration). item_cutoffs data.frame per-item cutoff summaries: Item, infit_low, infit_high, outfit_low, outfit_high. Bounds computed using method specified cutoff_method. actual_iterations Number successful iterations. sample_n Number complete cases used. sample_summary Summary statistics estimated person parameters. item_names Character vector item names data. cutoff_method method used compute cutoffs ("hdci" "quantile"). hdci_width HDCI width used (meaningful cutoff_method = "hdci").

title: Simulation-Based Infit MSQ Cutoff Determination — RMinfitcutoff
text: simulation iteration, person parameters (thetas) resampled replacement ML estimates, response data simulated Rasch model, model refitted, conditional infit outfit MSQ statistics computed via iarm::out_infit(). distribution statistics across iterations provides empirical critical values per item. Failed iterations (e.g., due convergence issues degenerate data) silently discarded. Supports dichotomous data (via eRm::RM() psychotools::rrm()) polytomous data (via eRm::PCM() internal partial credit score simulator). Parallel processing provided mirai package (optional). Install install.packages("mirai") enable parallelisation. iarm package must installed (Suggests, Imports).

title: Simulation-Based Infit MSQ Cutoff Determination — RMinfitcutoff
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(42) sim_data <- as.data.frame(   matrix(sample(0:1, 200 * 10, replace = TRUE), nrow = 200, ncol = 10) ) colnames(sim_data) <- paste0("Item", 1:10)  # Run 100 iterations sequentially for a quick demo cutoff_res <- RMinfitcutoff(sim_data, iterations = 100, parallel = FALSE,                             seed = 42) cutoff_res$item_cutoffs  # Use the cutoffs in RMiteminfit() RMiteminfit(sim_data) } # }

what: Plot Distribution of Simulated Infit and Outfit MSQ Values — RMinfitcutoffPlot
title: Plot Distribution of Simulated Infit and Outfit MSQ Values — RMinfitcutoffPlot
text: Visualises distribution simulation-based conditional item fit values RMinfitcutoff, optionally overlaying observed item fit original data.

title: Plot Distribution of Simulated Infit and Outfit MSQ Values — RMinfitcutoffPlot
code: RMinfitcutoffPlot(simfit, data, output = "infit")

title: Plot Distribution of Simulated Infit and Outfit MSQ Values — RMinfitcutoffPlot
text: simfit return value RMinfitcutoff (list components results, item_cutoffs, actual_iterations, sample_n, item_names). data Optional. data.frame matrix item responses computing overlaying observed conditional item fit values. Items must scored starting 0 (non-negative integers). provided, plot includes orange diamond markers observed infit/outfit MSQ alongside simulated distribution, plus segment summaries cutoff intervals. output Character string. Either "infit" (default) show infit panel, "outfit" show outfit panel, "" show infit outfit side side (requires patchwork package data supplied).

title: Plot Distribution of Simulated Infit and Outfit MSQ Values — RMinfitcutoffPlot
text: ggplot object (patchwork object output = "" data supplied).

title: Plot Distribution of Simulated Infit and Outfit MSQ Values — RMinfitcutoffPlot
text: Uses ggdist::stat_dotsinterval() (data supplied) ggdist::stat_dots() (data supplied) point_interval = "median_hdci" .width = c(0.66, 0.999). data supplied, function plots simulated MSQ distributions dot-interval plots using ggdist::stat_dotsinterval() median Highest Density Continuous Interval (HDCI) summaries, faceted statistic (InfitMSQ / OutfitMSQ). data supplied, function: Fits Rasch model (eRm::RM() dichotomous data eRm::PCM() polytomous data) computes observed conditional infit outfit MSQ via iarm::out_infit(). Overlays observed fit values orange diamond markers simulated distributions. Shows per-item cutoff intervals (simfit$item_cutoffs) black line segments, thicker segments 66% HDCI range black dots median. ggplot2, ggdist, optionally patchwork packages must installed (Suggests, Imports).

title: Plot Distribution of Simulated Infit and Outfit MSQ Values — RMinfitcutoffPlot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(42) sim_data <- as.data.frame(   matrix(sample(0:1, 200 * 10, replace = TRUE), nrow = 200, ncol = 10) ) colnames(sim_data) <- paste0("Item", 1:10)  # Run simulation cutoff_res <- RMinfitcutoff(sim_data, iterations = 100, parallel = FALSE,                             seed = 42)  # Simulated distribution only (infit + outfit faceted) RMinfitcutoffPlot(cutoff_res)  # With observed fit overlaid (infit only, the default) RMinfitcutoffPlot(cutoff_res, data = sim_data)  # Both infit and outfit panels side by side RMinfitcutoffPlot(cutoff_res, data = sim_data, output = "both") } # }

title: Conditional Item Infit MSQ — RMiteminfit
text: Computes conditional infit mean-square (MSQ) statistics item using iarm::out_infit(), enriched item locations relative sample mean person location.

title: Conditional Item Infit MSQ — RMiteminfit
code: RMiteminfit(data, cutoff = NULL, output = "kable", sort)

title: Conditional Item Infit MSQ — RMiteminfit
text: data data.frame matrix item responses. Items must scored starting 0 (non-negative integers). Missing values (NA) allowed, least one complete case (row NA) must present. cutoff Optional. Default NULL (cutoff applied, behaviour identical current version). Can : return value RMinfitcutoff (list $item_cutoffs): data.frame extracted automatically number simulation iterations, cutoff_method, hdci_width included kable caption. $item_cutoffs data.frame RMinfitcutoff directly: must columns Item, infit_low, infit_high. provided, adds columns Infit_low, Infit_high, Flagged (logical; TRUE Infit_MSQ falls outside credible range) result. output Character string controlling return value. Either "kable" (default) formatted knitr::kable() table, "dataframe" underlying data.frame. sort Optional character string. sort = "infit", rows sorted Infit_MSQ descending order output.

title: Conditional Item Infit MSQ — RMiteminfit
text: output = "kable": knitr_kable object (plain text table via format = "pipe") columns "Item", "Infit MSQ", "Relative location", caption showing number complete cases. cutoff provided, columns "Infit low", "Infit high", "Flagged" also included, caption notes simulation-based cutoffs. output = "dataframe": data.frame columns Item, Infit_MSQ, Relative_location. cutoff provided, columns Infit_low, Infit_high, Flagged also included (inserted Infit_MSQ, Relative_location).

title: Conditional Item Infit MSQ — RMiteminfit
text: Infit MSQ weighted fit statistic emphasises deviations near item location. Values close 1.0 indicate good fit. Values substantially 1.0 suggest underfit (unexpected responses), values substantially 1.0 suggest overfit (overly predictable responses). definition "substantially" depends several factors sample size, needs determined simulation using RMinfitcutoff. general rule--thumb value correct. Conditional infit MSQ statistics computed via iarm::out_infit(), uses conditional distribution sufficient statistics (Müller, 2020). complete cases (rows without NA) used conditional fit calculation. dichotomous data (maximum score = 1), Rasch model fitted via eRm::RM(). Item locations negative beta parameters. Person locations estimated via eRm::person.parameter(). polytomous data (maximum score > 1), Partial Credit Model fitted via eRm::PCM(). Item average locations taken "Location" column threshold parameter table returned eRm::thresholds(); column absent, row means threshold columns used instead. Person locations estimated via eRm::person.parameter(). Relative item location defined item's average location minus sample mean person location, providing measure item targeting. iarm package must installed (Suggests, Imports).

title: Conditional Item Infit MSQ — RMiteminfit

title: Q3 Residual Correlations for Local Dependence Assessment — RMlocdepQ3

title: Partial Gamma DIF Analysis — RMpartgamDIF
text: Partial gamma (Bjorner et al., 1998) measures association item response exogenous grouping variable, controlling total score. Values near 0 indicate DIF. Recommended interpretive thresholds (Bjorner et al., 1998): negligible DIF: gamma within \([-0.21, 0.21]\), gamma significantly different 0. Slight moderate DIF: gamma within \([-0.31, 0.31]\) (outside \([-0.21, 0.21]\)), significantly outside \([-0.21, 0.21]\). Moderate large DIF: gamma outside \([-0.31, 0.31]\), significantly outside \([-0.21, 0.21]\). iarm package must installed (Suggests, Imports).

what: easyRasch2: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R — easyRasch2-package
title: easyRasch2: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R — easyRasch2-package
text: Provides functions simplify Rasch measurement theory analysis workflows, local dependence assessment item fit, person fit, differential item functioning, targeting, reliability. Uses Conditional Maximum Likelihood (CML) estimation via 'eRm' 'psychotools' item parameters Weighted Likelihood Estimation (WLE) person parameters, 'mirt' residual correlation analysis. Designed easy integration 'Quarto' documents using 'knitr::kable()' table output 'ggplot2' figures.

title: easyRasch2: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R — easyRasch2-package
text: Maintainer: Magnus Johansson pgmj@pm.(ORCID) contributors: Nicklas Korsell (PCM simulation code) [contributor]

title: easyRasch2 0.5.1
text: New function RMinfitcutoffPlot() illustrate distribution simulated conditional item infit MSQ values together observed value.

title: easyRasch2 0.5.0
text: RMinfitcutoff() gains cutoff_method hdci_width parameters: default (cutoff_method = "hdci", hdci_width = 0.999), per-item cutoff intervals now computed using Highest Density Interval via ggdist::hdci() (99.9% HDCI). Set cutoff_method = "quantile" restore previous behaviour (2.5th/97.5th percentiles). ggdist package required cutoff_method = "hdci" (added Suggests). returned list now also includes cutoff_method hdci_width fields. RMiteminfit() caption updated: cutoff return value RMinfitcutoff(), kable caption now states cutoff method, e.g. "Cutoff values based 250 simulation iterations (99.9% HDCI)." "Cutoff values based 250 simulation iterations (2.5th/97.5th percentile).".

title: easyRasch2 0.4.0
text: RMiteminfit() gains optional cutoff parameter: Accepts return value RMinfitcutoff() ($item_cutoffs data.frame directly). provided, per-item cutoff boundaries (Infit_low, Infit_high) logical Flagged column added "dataframe" "kable" output. kable caption includes number simulation iterations available. New RMinfitcutoff(): Simulation-based (parametric bootstrap) cutoff determination [RMiteminfit()]. Supports dichotomous polytomous data. Optional parallel processing via mirai (falls back sequential installed). Returns per-item 2.5th 97.5th percentiles simulated infit outfit MSQ distributions ($item_cutoffs), together full iteration-level results ($results). Requires iarm package (Suggests).

title: easyRasch2 0.3.0
text: New RMiteminfit(): Computes conditional infit MSQ statistics item via iarm::out_infit(), enriched item locations relative sample mean person location. Supports dichotomous (Rasch model via eRm::RM()) polytomous (Partial Credit Model via eRm::PCM()) data. Requires iarm package (Suggests). Output options: "kable" (default, plain-text knitr::kable()) "dataframe". Optional sort = "infit" sorts infit MSQ descending. complete cases used conditional fit calculation. New RMitemrestscore(): Computes observed model-expected item-restscore correlations via iarm::item_restscore(), enriched absolute differences observed expected values, item average locations, item locations relative sample mean person location. Supports dichotomous (Rasch model via eRm::RM()) polytomous (Partial Credit Model via eRm::PCM()) data. Requires iarm package (Suggests). Output options: "kable" (default, plain-text knitr::kable()) "dataframe". Optional sort = "diff" sorts absolute difference descending.

title: easyRasch2 0.2.0
text: RMlocdepQ3(): cutoff now optional (default NULL). omitted, raw Q3 residual correlation matrix returned without dynamic cut-applied. provided, dynamic cut-(mean Q3 + cutoff) shown kable caption . Supersedes requirement always supply cutoff value (PRs #2 #3). New RMlocdepQ3cutoff(): Simulation-based (parametric bootstrap) cutoff determination RMlocdepQ3(). Supports dichotomous polytomous data. Optional parallel processing via mirai (falls back sequential installed). $suggested_cutoff 99th percentile simulated Q3 max–mean distribution. New internal helpers R/utils-simulation.R: sim_poly_item(), sim_partial_score(), extract_item_thresholds(). Dependencies: eRm, psychotools (>= 0.7-3), parallel, utils moved/added Imports. mirai added Suggests.

title: easyRasch2 0.1.0
text: Initial package structure. Added RMlocdepQ3() Yen’s Q3 residual correlation analysis local dependence.
